notes: Odds compiled from DraftKings, FanDuel, BetMGM, Caesars, VegasInsider, Covers, OddShark. Conference tournaments in full swing — ACC quarterfinals completed (Duke survives FSU 80-79, Clemson upsets UNC 80-79), SEC second round completed (Auburn eliminated by Tennessee 72-62), Big Ten/Big 12/Big East quarterfinals and semifinals on March 13. Duke remains consensus #1 favorite. Projected No. 1 seeds unchanged: Duke, Michigan, Arizona, Florida. Auburn bubble burst after SEC loss — may be first 16-loss at-large if selected. Oklahoma surging onto bubble (42% forecast). Selection Sunday March 15.
changes_since_march12: Duke odds stable at +325 consensus after surviving ACC quarterfinal scare vs FSU (80-79)
Michigan stable at +335; faces Ohio State in Big Ten quarterfinals March 13
Arizona stable at +475; faces Iowa State in Big 12 semifinals March 13
Florida stable at +675-775 range; faces Kentucky in SEC quarterfinals March 13
BetMGM now showing Duke +320, Michigan +325, Arizona +475, Florida +775, Houston +1000
Auburn bubble burst — eliminated from SEC tournament by Tennessee 72-62; blew 10-point lead with 10 min left (20-0 Tennessee run); now 17-16, ESPN projects first four OUT
Oklahoma surging — beat South Carolina and Texas A&M (83-63) in SEC tournament; bubble odds up to 42% per forecast models; replaced SMU as last team in
ACC quarterfinal results: Duke 80 FSU 79, Virginia 81 NC State 74, Miami 78 Louisville 73, Clemson 80 UNC 79
SEC second round results: Kentucky 78 Missouri 72, Tennessee 72 Auburn 62, Ole Miss 76 Georgia 72, Oklahoma 83 Texas A&M 63
Big Ten third round results: Ohio State 72 Iowa 69, Wisconsin 85 Washington 82, Purdue 81 Northwestern 68, UCLA 72 Rutgers 59
Big East quarterfinal results: St. John's 85 Providence 72, Seton Hall 72 Creighton 61, UConn 93 Xavier 68, Georgetown 78 Villanova 64
Big 12 quarterfinal results: Arizona 81 UCF 59, Iowa State beat Texas Tech by 22, Houston 73 BYU 66, Kansas 78 TCU 73

New auto-bids clinched since March 12: Gonzaga (WCC), Northern Iowa (MVC), Idaho (Big Sky — first bid since 1990), High Point (Big South), Hofstra (CAA), Troy (Sun Belt), Furman (SoCon), Wright State (Horizon), Siena (MAAC), LIU (NEC), Lehigh (Patriot), McNeese State (Southland), North Dakota State (Summit), Tennessee State (OVC), Queens (ASUN)
Bubble weakest in recent memory per CBS Sports — teams with prolonged slumps getting grace because others are also losing

projected_no1_seeds.source: ESPN Bracketology, Andy Katz (NCAA.com), FOX Sports (Mike DeCourcy), CBS Sports — as of March 13
projected_no1_seeds.notes: Unchanged from March 12. Duke, Michigan, Arizona have separated themselves. Florida newest addition after closing regular season in dominant fashion. UConn still in No. 1 seed debate per CBS but dropped from consensus No. 1 seed group. Conference tournament results could still reshuffle — CBS notes conference tournaments could decide top seeds.

public_betting_data.source: Multiple sportsbooks, Yahoo Sports, Covers — as of March 13

key_moves_since_march12: Championship futures mostly stable as conference tournaments proceed — no major shifts in top 10
Auburn drifting further: +50000 to +75000 after SEC tournament elimination
Oklahoma shortening: +50000 to +40000 on SEC tournament surge
Texas drifting: +22500 to +25000 after SEC tournament first-round loss to Ole Miss
Wisconsin vs Illinois Big Ten quarterfinal: Line moved from Illinois -8.5 to -7.5 on sharp action favoring Wisconsin; 96-97% public money on Wisconsin
BetMGM Duke at +320, Caesars Duke at +315 — slightly shorter than DK/FD
Purdue at Caesars +5000 vs BetMGM +2000 — widest spread among books for any top-15 team
Dan Hurley gave 'unfortunate update' for Big East tournament per Newsweek — potential UConn injury concern to monitor
value_plays_identified: Illinois at +1600 — still best value among teams longer than +1000 per multiple analysts (unchanged)
Michigan State at +4000 — most Quad 1 wins outside top 4 (unchanged)
Florida at +675 — defending champ, 10-game win streak, projected No. 1 seed (unchanged)
St. John's at +2500 — Big East #1 seed, if they beat UConn for Big East title could shorten further
Oklahoma at +40000 — surging on bubble, 8 wins in 10, replaced SMU as last team in

bubble_watch_march_13.source: ESPN Bubble Watch, CBS Bracketology, FOX Sports projections — March 13
bubble_watch_march_13.notes: Weakest bubble in recent memory per CBS Sports. Teams with prolonged late-season slumps getting grace because others also losing. Auburn (17-16) would be first 16-loss at-large team in history if selected. Oklahoma surging — replaced SMU as last team in after SEC tournament wins. VCU A-10 game March 13 is critical. Indiana projected OUT by FOX Sports.
key_bubble_games_march_13: VCU in A-10 tournament — result could determine at-large status
Mountain West tournament — bubble implications for multiple teams

early_round_lines._note: Selection Sunday is March 15, 2026. R64 spreads/moneylines posted after bracket announcement. First Four March 17-18, Round of 64 March 19-20.

de_vig_methodology._note: Raw implied probabilities from American odds sum to >100% due to vig. For simulation, de-vig using power method or multiplicative approach.
de_vig_methodology.recommended_de_vig: Divide each team's implied_prob by sum of all implied_probs to normalize to 1.0
